title: tetragonia macrocarpa
intro: Tetragonia macrocarpa, also known as New Zealand spinach or kaki, is a perennial succulent plant native to New Zealand. It has been used for centuries in traditional Maori medicine, and its medicinal properties are attributed to its high concentration of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Tetragonia macrocarpa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to salads and used as a cooking green.
